message:
test(fuzz): extend crdt_merge_invariants to 3-replica all-permutation merge coverage

Strengthen the CRDT merge fuzz target to assert convergence not just
between two replicas but across all six 3-replica merge orderings.

- Replicas enum gains a third variant (A/B/C replacing Left/Right);
  `map_for` now maps each into a distinct slot index.
- New `merged_in_order(replicas, [i, j, k])` clones the replica at
  the first index and merges the other two in the requested order.
- New `assert_merge_invariants(replicas, label)` runs all six
  permutations through `merged_in_order` and asserts every result
  is byte-equal, with `label` carried into panic messages so a
  failure points at the offending CRDT type. This proves
  associativity + commutativity + idempotency simultaneously
  (rather than just commutativity for two replicas).
- BTreeSet pulled in to support deterministic state comparison
  inside the assertion path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
